parsed_findings.chain_steps: Attacker controls `dir` in the request -> application builds `q` using an f-string -> database executes attacker-influenced `ORDER BY` clause -> attacker gains SQL injection impact.
parsed_findings.impact: An attacker may execute arbitrary SQL (e.g., data exfiltration or destructive actions) depending on the database driver and SQL capabilities.
parsed_findings.proof: Supplying `dir=asc;SELECT%201--` makes the constructed query include attacker-controlled SQL after `{sort}` in `ORDER BY {sort} {direction}`.
parsed_findings.repro_steps: Send a request like `GET /?sort=name&dir=asc%3BSELECT%201--` and observe altered query behavior or SQL execution errors.
parsed_findings.vuln_claim: The `dir` request parameter is interpolated into the SQL `ORDER BY` clause without validation, enabling SQL injection.
